mechanism_narrative: A consistent upward trend in industrial production indicates sustained economic expansion, raising corporate earnings expectations and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. The signal is always-on because production growth is a persistent positive factor.
failure_condition: The strategy produces negative average forward returns or a Sharpe ratio below 0 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above the 5% threshold, it signals the onset of a labor market deterioration that typically precedes or accompanies a recession, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of instances after UNRATE crosses above 5.0 over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decline in housing starts and rise in unemployment revenue strong recessionary pressure: collapsing residential investment and weakening labor market jointly reduce aggregate demand and corporate earnings, generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint trigger fires fewer than 5 times in sample, or positive returns occur in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle, declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate on future earnings, raising equity valuations and producing positive forward returns. The mechanism is regime-conditional: in easing, rate declines are positive; in tightening, rate increases are negative.
failure_condition: Forward returns in easing-triggered periods are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the strategy underperforms a buy-and-hold over a full cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Growing real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings. A consistently rising trend in sales supports positive one-month equity returns.
failure_condition: The strategy produces negative average forward returns or the correlation between RSAFS growth and SPX returns is non-significant (p > 0.10) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp one-month decline in industrial production by more than 1% signals a sudden supply disruption or demand collapse, prompting immediate negative market reaction over the following week as investors reassess economic weakness.
failure_condition: Rank IC over 5-day forward returns is less than -0.03 after trigger events, or the hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation is elevated (above 210 index level) and the unemployment rate simultaneously rises above 5%, the economy faces stagflationary conditions: rising prices with weakening growth. This joint signal erodes equity valuations through both earnings compression and multiple contraction, leading to negative returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint trigger occurs fewer than 3 times in the test sample, or the average forward return is not negative (one-sided test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5%), any further rise in unemployment is a particularly sensitive signal of recession onset because it breaks from a strong labor market. Such rises predict negative forward returns over the next month as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.2% point in one month
failure_condition: Signal produces positive returns in more than 40% of triggers during low unemployment episodes, or information ratio < 0.2 over rolling 2-year windows.
